Fills an array with numbers from 0 to 4999, repeated three times.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the creation and population of arrays within a loop. It contains a nested loop structure where the outer loop runs three times, and for each iteration, it initializes an empty array `a`. The inner loop iterates 5000 times, populating the array `a` with integers from 0 to 4999. This script illustrates basic array manipulation and loop constructs in JavaScript, focusing on the repetitive creation and filling of arrays.
